evals.prompt: Using evals/files/object-duplicates.graphql, identify object types with exactly the same fields and types, consolidate safe duplicates automatically, and preserve client-facing compatibility by default. Show what you changed and what you skipped.
evals.expected_output: Finds exact duplicate object type shapes, performs only non-breaking consolidations, and reports changed and skipped groups with reasons.
expectations: Output identifies at least one duplicate object-type group using exact field/type matching criteria.
Output explicitly states that non-breaking behavior is the default and that breaking merges require opt-in.
Output includes a per-group disposition (merged, implementation-only, or skipped) with rationale.

evals.prompt: Using evals/files/input-duplicates.graphql, scan for duplicate input object types with identical fields and types, merge only safe duplicates, and avoid any breaking changes to public API contracts.
evals.expected_output: Detects duplicate input objects separately from object types, applies safe merges only, and explains skipped breaking-risk cases.
expectations: Output treats input object types as a separate duplicate-detection category from object types.
Output avoids cross-kind merges (object to input or input to object).
Output flags any public-contract-breaking merge candidates as skipped pending explicit approval.

evals.prompt: Using evals/files/non-exact-shapes.graphql, consolidate only exact duplicates and explain why near matches with directive/default-value differences were not merged.
evals.expected_output: Consolidates only strict exact-shape duplicates and rejects near matches where directives/defaults differ.
expectations: Output requires exact-shape equality and does not merge near duplicates.
Output explains that directive/default-value mismatches are excluded from automatic consolidation by default.
Output provides clear skipped reasons for non-exact groups.
